19	\section{Setup and motivation}
20	
21	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
22	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
23	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
24	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
25	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
26	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
27	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
28	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
29	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
30	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
31	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
32	
33	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
34	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
35	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
36	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
37	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
38	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
39	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
40	\[
41	  \begin{array}{ccc}
42	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
43	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
44	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
45	  \end{array}
46	\]
47	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
48	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
49	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
50	
51	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
52

92	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),

96	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials

99	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open

101	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
102	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).

169	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
170	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
171	
172	It is convenient to factor the construction of
173	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
174	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
175	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
176	
177	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
178	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
179	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
180	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
181	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
182	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
183	\[
184	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
185	    \;=\;
186	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
187	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
188	\]
189	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
190	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
191	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
192	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
193	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
194	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
195	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
196	
197	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
198	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
199	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
200	\[
201	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
202	  \qquad
203	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
204	\]
205	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
206	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
207	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
208	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
209	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
210	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
211	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
212	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
213	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
214	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
215	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
216	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
217	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
218	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
219	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
220	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
221	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
222	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
223	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
224	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
225	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
226	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
227	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
228	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
229	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
230	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
231	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
232	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
233	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
234	
235	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
236	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
237	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
238	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
239	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
240	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
241	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
242	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
243	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
244	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
245	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
246	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
247	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
248	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
249	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
250	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
251	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
252	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
253	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
254	
255	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
256	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
257	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
258	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
259	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
260

401	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then

493	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,

511	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
512	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
513	
514	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
515	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
516	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
517	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
518	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
519	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
520	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
521	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
522	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
523	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
524	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
525	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
526	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
527	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
528	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.

1347	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1348	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1349	
1350	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1351	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1352	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1353	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1354	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1355	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1356	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1357	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1358	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1359	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1360	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1361	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1362	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1363	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1364	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1365	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1366	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1367	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1368	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1369	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1370	\(\delta\)-functor identification.

638	/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
639	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
640	
641	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
642	— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
643	on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
644	of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
645	`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
646	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.
647	
648	The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
649	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
650	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
651	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
652	section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
653	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
654	single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.

658	theorem ses_cech_h1 {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
659	    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
660	    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
661	    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
662	    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
663	    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
664	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
665	    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
666	    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
667	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
668	    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
669	    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
670	    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
671	    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
672	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
673	    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
674	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
675	  classical
676	  -- single-index local sections, recast in ⨅-coordinates
677	  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
678	    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
679	    with hsLoc'coord
680	  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
681	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
682	  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
683	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
684	  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
685	  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
686	    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
687	  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
688	    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
689	  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
690	      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
691	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
692	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
693	    intro σ
694	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ
695	    rw [hpe0] at happ
696	    exact happ
697	  -- `gπ` carries a single ⨅-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
698	  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
699	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
700	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
701	    intro ρ h
702	    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
703	      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
704	    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
705	  -- `gπ` kills the differences
706	  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
707	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
708	    intro σ
709	    rw [hdG, map_sum]
710	    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
711	        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
712	          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
713	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
714	        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
715	            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (σ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (σ 0)))).op) s := by
716	      intro i
717	      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1

3026	\begin{lemma}
3027	[Degree-zero absolute cohomology is global sections]
3028	  \label{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero}

3030	  \uses{def:absolute_cohomology, lem:jshriek_corepr, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}
3031	  \textit{Project specialization of Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib} composed with
3032	  corepresentability.}
3033	  For every \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) and open \(U \subseteq X\) there is an additive
3034	  isomorphism
3035	  \[
3036	    H^0(U, \mathcal{F}) \;=\; \operatorname{Ext}^0(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F})
3037	      \;\cong\; \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}).
3038	  \]
3039	\end{lemma}
3040	\begin{proof}
3041	  \uses{lem:jshriek_corepr, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}
3042	  Compose the degree-zero Ext comparison
3043	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
3044	  \operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F})\)
3045	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}) with the corepresentability isomorphism
3046	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}) \cong \Gamma(U,
3047	  \mathcal{F})\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr}. Both are additive, so is the composite.
3048	\end{proof}

3051	[Naturality of \(H^0 \cong \Gamma\) in the coefficient sheaf]
3052	  \label{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural}

3054	  \uses{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:jshriek_corepr}
3055	  \textit{Project-bespoke obligation.}
3056	  The isomorphism \(H^0(U, -) \cong \Gamma(U, -)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero} is
3057	  natural in the coefficient sheaf: for a morphism \(g : \mathcal{F}_1 \to \mathcal{F}_2\) in
3058	  \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) the square
3059	  \[
3060	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3061	      H^0(U, \mathcal{F}_1) & \longrightarrow & H^0(U, \mathcal{F}_2)\\
3062	      \cong\downarrow & & \downarrow\cong\\
3063	      \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}_1) & \xrightarrow{\;g_U\;} & \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F}_2)
3064	    \end{array}
3065	  \]
3066	  commutes, where the top arrow is \(H^0(U, g)\), the functorial action of \(g\) on
3067	  \(\operatorname{Ext}^0\) in the second variable, and the bottom arrow is the sections map
3068	  \(g_U = g(U)\).
3069	\end{lemma}
3070	\begin{proof}
3071	  \uses{lem:absolute_cohomology_zero, lem:jshriek_corepr}
3072	  Under the degree-zero Ext comparison, post-composition by the degree-zero Ext class of \(g\)
3073	  corresponds to post-composition by \(g\) in \(\operatorname{Hom}_{X.\mathrm{Modules}}(j_!
3074	  \mathcal{O}_U, -)\); under the corepresentability isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:jshriek_corepr}
3075	  (which is natural in the second variable, being a composite of the sheafification adjunction and
3076	  the free–Yoneda evaluation, both natural), this in turn corresponds to applying \(g\) on sections
3077	  over \(U\), i.e. \(g_U\). Hence the square commutes. In particular, when \(g_U\) is surjective so
3078	  is \(H^0(U, g)\) — the transfer used by the surjectivity step of
3079	  Lemma~\ref{lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing}.
3080	\end{proof}

3240	  (-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) of section groups over basic opens. For each basic open
